summary: Carpal tunnel syndrome is caused by compression of the median nerve beneath the flexor retinaculum.
Carpal tunnel syndrome is caused by compression of the ulnar nerve in Guyon's canal.
Carpal tunnel syndrome results from radial nerve compression at the wrist.
Wrist drop results from radial nerve injury causing inability to extend the wrist and fingers.
Wrist drop results from median nerve injury causing inability to flex the wrist.
Wrist drop results from ulnar nerve injury causing weakness of grip.
Claw hand deformity results from ulnar nerve injury with hyperextension at MCP and flexion at IP joints.
Claw hand results from median nerve injury with inability to oppose the thumb.
Claw hand results from radial nerve injury with inability to extend the fingers.
Flexor carpi ulnaris is the only superficial forearm flexor supplied by the ulnar nerve, not the median nerve.
Pronator teres is the only superficial forearm flexor supplied by the ulnar nerve.
Palmaris longus is the only superficial flexor supplied by the ulnar nerve.
The anatomical snuffbox has the scaphoid bone on its floor, making it tender in scaphoid fractures.
The anatomical snuffbox has the lunate bone on its floor.
The anatomical snuffbox has the trapezium on its floor.
Palmar interossei adduct the fingers (PAD) and dorsal interossei abduct them (DAB).
Palmar interossei abduct the fingers and dorsal interossei adduct them.
Both palmar and dorsal interossei are supplied by the median nerve.
The thenar muscles are supplied by the recurrent branch of the median nerve.
The thenar muscles are supplied by the deep branch of the ulnar nerve.
The thenar muscles are supplied by the anterior interosseous nerve.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review forearm and hand anatomy and try again.
